description: Weather state machine test vectors — verifies the single weatherIntensity float (0-2) drives correct derived parameters, rain particles, ground wetness hysteresis, and state transitions. All interpolation uses lerp3(t, a, b, c).

vectors.description: Valid weather states: sunny (0), cloudy (1), rainy (2). The state machine transitions between these by ramping weatherIntensity.

expected.note: weatherIntensity is a continuous float. States are thresholds: 0=sunny, 1=cloudy, 2=rainy. Transitions select a random different target.

vectors.description: Transition rate: WEATHER_TRANSITION_RATE = 0.0083 units/second. Approximately 2 minutes to traverse 1 intensity unit.

expected.note: 1.0 / 0.0083 = ~120.5 seconds per intensity unit. Each full state transition (e.g. sunny->cloudy) takes approximately 2 minutes.

vectors.description: Derived parameters at intensity=0 (sunny): all storm effects absent, baseline values.
Derived parameters at intensity=1.0 (cloudy): overcast sky, no rain yet.
Derived parameters at intensity=2.0 (rainy): full storm, maximum rain.

expected.note: Rain appears as hair-thin vertical streaks (extreme X/Y aspect ratio ~80:0.8). Translucent blue-grey, NOT round dots.

vectors.description: Hold time range: each weather state holds for WEATHER_HOLD_MIN to WEATHER_HOLD_MAX seconds before transitioning.

expected.note: After reaching target intensity, the system holds for 3-8 minutes before selecting a new random target.

vectors.description: Ground wetness hysteresis: wets faster than it dries. Wetting only occurs during rain (rainIntensity > 0).

expected.note: Wetting rate is ~2x drying rate. Ground stays wet longer than it takes to get wet, creating realistic puddle persistence. Wetting is scaled by rainIntensity; drying is constant.

vectors.description: Intermediate intensity: at weatherIntensity=0.5 (halfway between sunny and cloudy), all lerp3 values should be midpoint between sunny and cloudy values.

expected.note: lerp3(0.5, a, b, c) = lerp(a, b, 0.5) for all parameters. Rain is 0 because max(0, 0.5-1) = 0.

vectors.description: Intermediate intensity: at weatherIntensity=1.5 (halfway between cloudy and rainy), lerp3 produces mid-storm values.

expected.note: lerp3(1.5, a, b, c) = lerp(b, c, 0.5). Rain = max(0, 1.5-1) = 0.5. Storm is building.
